cards.title: Your AI Dev Tools Are Broken: The Untapped Opportunity in Reliability and Local Control
cards.thesis: Builders are increasingly relying on AI agents (AI assistants that can perform complex tasks autonomously, often by using 'tool calling' which lets them interact with other software) for coding and design, but they're hitting major roadblocks with existing platforms. Users are losing access to projects, encountering buggy interfaces, and struggling with unreliable services. This creates a massive opening for tools that prioritize local control, data ownership, and rock-solid reliability, especially as smaller, faster AI models become more common.

evidence.text: We open-sourced Needle, a 26M parameter function-calling (tool use) model. It runs at 6000 tok/s prefill and 1200 tok/s decode on consumer devices. We were always frustrated by the little effort made towards building agentic models that run on budget phones.

I wanted to try codex after 5 months of claude code max subscription. And then I went back to my previous projects on claude design only to realize I don't have access to them anymore. This is a first. I never lost access to any of my past sessions because I unsubscribed in any of the LLM apps.

The Claude app and Claude Code have been an unusable and buggy mess for me lately, has anyone else been experiencing this? Most of my messages get swallowed after sending them or the responses get interrupted or dropped. Sometimes entire conversations disappear from the sidebar only to reappear later.

We're building database sandboxes for you and your coding agents. In the last two years coding agents have gotten dramatically more capable at handling complex engineering tasks. But without access to a realistic sandbox at the DB layer for testing, they ship garbage that can take down pro[duction systems].

cards.opportunity: The biggest headache for builders using AI tools right now is losing projects or dealing with buggy interfaces, like the 'unusable mess' people are seeing with Claude. Forget building another AI coding tool; instead, create a local 'agent session vault' that automatically snapshots an AI agent's entire workspace – code, database changes, and conversation history – letting users switch between LLMs or even unsubscribe without ever losing their progress. This is critical now that smaller, local AI models are making on-device agent work a reality, giving builders the control and reliability they desperately need.
